caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Sources measure client-side supply-chain adoption and applications, not task shares inside NAICS 541614 consultancies.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied role mix is four-digit 5416 and likely understates logisticians, industrial engineers and field implementation staff.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Adoption pace, fee capture and terminal demand are not used to inflate these task fractions.
inputs.pi_dist: No source measures the fee-model mix for the target EBITDA band or this NAICS code.
inputs.pi_dist: Gainshare prevalence in freight audit is not quantified here and therefore receives no direct observed weight.
inputs.pi_moat: Equiteq describes preferred consulting attributes broadly rather than measuring logistics-consulting switching costs.
inputs.pi_moat: Physical implementation supports terminal demand separately and is not itself counted as capture.
inputs.t50_years: Client-side adoption is a proxy for consultant delivery adoption.
inputs.t50_years: The 82% figure is a five-year expectation, not an observed diffusion curve.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: This is observed for surveyed supply-chain organizations, not US lower-middle-market 541614 consulting firms.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Use of any AI is broader than effective adoption across consulting delivery tasks.
inputs.historical_analogs: This qualitative analog does not set role fractions or adoption timing by itself.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: This is not evidence of a sector succession wave.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Individual firms may still have material succession risk.
inputs.active_consolidators: This is not a direct count of bidders for 541614 firms.
inputs.active_consolidators: Used only for buyability, not adoption or task exposure.
inputs.buy_mult: BizBuySell's 2.72x service-business figure is discretionary earnings for much smaller firms, not EBITDA for the target band.
inputs.buy_mult: Partner compensation, earnouts and cyclicality require normalization.
inputs.exit_mult: No direct 541614 transaction multiple supports the selected expansion.
inputs.exit_mult: Exit uplift is conditional on reduced founder dependence and stronger recurring or gainshare revenue.
inputs.pricing_structure: No direct evidence quantifies gainshare, fixed-fee or T&M revenue in NAICS 541614.
Validator: Supply-chain operator adoption and the four-digit 5416 role mix support direction, not six-digit 541614 consultant task shares or diffusion timing.
Validator: The six-buyer estimate and 6.0x entry/7.5x exit case are judgmental assumptions; the cited M&A evidence supplies only broad direction and a mismatched micro-market comparator.
Validator: Separate contract/data-based retention from the survival of human implementation work before treating the 0.45 moat estimate as capture.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $253,468 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541614); class <5: 5,724 firms, avg revenue/firm $515,407 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $80,661 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 693 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,238,423 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $350,313 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 419 firms, avg revenue/firm $4,800,974 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $751,352 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 481 firms, avg revenue/firm $10,617,393 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,661,622 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 162 firms, avg revenue/firm $30,714,025 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $4,806,745 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 151 firms, avg revenue/firm $55,737,146 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $8,722,863 -> IN $1-10M band; n_band = 794

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Supply-chain AI use cases directly affect analysis, prediction, recommendation and documentation, while physical implementation and organizational change remain outside the readily automated task set.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541614 ($14,727,537,868) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541614 ($25,011,973,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics and exact-artifact binding pass. The directional logistics-consulting thesis remains useful, but consultant-specific adoption, fee mix, buyer count, transaction multiples, and capture-versus-terminal separation require visible caveats.

sources.title: MHI and Deloitte 2025 Annual Industry Report release
sources.what_it_supports: Client-side supply-chain AI adoption: 28% in use and 82% expected within five years, plus use cases and data constraints.

sources.title: CSCMP State of Logistics Report
sources.what_it_supports: Current logistics context showing uneven AI adoption, isolated point solutions and measured returns in specific applications.

sources.title: Deltek consulting pricing models guide
sources.what_it_supports: Use of time-and-materials, fixed, retainer and value/outcome-based consulting fee structures and their margin implications.

sources.title: Equiteq Specialty Consulting M&A Report — 2H 2025
sources.what_it_supports: Selective consulting M&A, buyer preference for specialization, proprietary data, platforms, recurring models and demonstrable AI execution.

sources.title: BizBuySell 2025 business valuation guide
sources.what_it_supports: Micro-business transaction context: 2025 service-business average earnings multiple of 2.72x on discretionary earnings, with explicit unit and size mismatch.

title: Process, Physical Distribution, and Logistics Consulting Services
